analytical_depth.rationale: Exceptional depth. The distinction between non-recurring employee cost leverage (pension arrears) and structural credit cost normalization is top-tier institutional analysis.

logical_consistency.rationale: Highly consistent narrative. The explanation of why SBI trades at a P/B discount to private peers despite a higher ROE (ROA gap + policy risk premium) is perfectly reasoned.

completeness.rationale: Covers all critical dimensions including SOTP, loan mix shifts, and deposit granularity. Minor gap in not explicitly modeling LCR (Liquidity Coverage Ratio) impacts in the base case, though it is flagged in open questions.

actionability.rationale: Provides a highly specific, catalyst-driven bull/bear framework with clear EPS/multiple assumptions and a practical quarterly monitoring checklist.

sector_framework.rationale: Flawless application of BFSI frameworks. Correctly discards EBITDA/FCF, utilizes the P/B-ROE justified valuation model, and focuses heavily on NIM, CASA, and asset quality metrics.

data_sourcing.rationale: Excellent inline citations and transparent manual math to compensate for a tool failure. However, failed to utilize available charting tools to visualize the data.

issues.issue: The agent had access to the 'render_chart' tool but never called it. A report detailing a massive 10-year turnaround (NPA crisis to record profits) heavily benefits from visual aids.
issues.suggestion: Update the system prompt to explicitly require the use of 'render_chart' for visualizing long-term P&L trends, asset quality improvements, or DuPont analysis in comprehensive business reports.

issues.issue: The 'mcp__business__calculate' tool failed repeatedly (stream closed), forcing the agent to perform manual calculations.
issues.suggestion: Investigate the MCP server hosting the calculate tool for timeout issues, connection stability, or payload size limits that cause stream closures.

strengths: Masterful SOTP (Sum of the Parts) analysis that strips out listed subsidiary value to reveal the implied valuation of the core banking business.
Deep understanding of PSU-specific dynamics, accurately identifying the 'policy risk premium' and the structural stickiness of government-mandated CASA deposits.
Excellent handling of a tool failure by explicitly showing the manual mathematical formulas used for derived metrics, ensuring complete transparency.
bfsi.summary: An outstanding, institutional-grade equity research report. The agent perfectly applied BFSI-specific valuation frameworks, demonstrated deep sector knowledge regarding PSU structural advantages/disadvantages, and provided highly actionable, catalyst-driven scenarios. The only notable flaw was the omission of visual charts despite having the tool available.
